dimensionDescriptions.adversary_modeling: How clearly your report defines the likely actor type and operating model under uncertainty.
dimensionDescriptions.intent: A reasoned statement of what the actor is trying to achieve and why it fits the evidence.
dimensionDescriptions.capability: Assessment of actor sophistication, access quality, and operational discipline.
dimensionDescriptions.targeting: Explanation of who or what is being prioritized and why that targeting is plausible.
dimensionDescriptions.next_move: Forecast of likely adversary behavior in the next 24–72 hours.
dimensionDescriptions.collection: Concrete collection actions that would materially reduce uncertainty.
dimensionDescriptions.mitigation: Immediate defensive actions matched to likely impact and operational constraints.
dimensionDescriptions.tradeoff: Acknowledgment of downside, friction, and second-order effects of chosen mitigations.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Rationale for risk level tied to likely impact and likelihood.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Rationale for confidence level tied to evidence quality and gaps.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary
reportSections.template: In 3–5 sentences, state the leading actor hypothesis, current risk, confidence, and the most important operational decision needed now.

reportSections.title: Threat Actor Assessment
reportSections.template: Summarize likely intent, capability, and targeting with concise justification from the scenario evidence.

reportSections.title: Evidence-to-Hypothesis Linkage
reportSections.template: Cite 3–5 specific evidence items from the simulation and explain how each supports, weakens, or complicates your selected hypothesis.

reportSections.title: Uncertainty and Collection Plan
reportSections.template: Identify major unknowns and list the top 1–2 collection actions most likely to change your assessment.

reportSections.title: Risk and Confidence Rationale
reportSections.template: Justify both risk and confidence levels using impact, likelihood, corroboration, and unresolved uncertainty.

reportSections.title: Recommendations and Tradeoffs
reportSections.template: Recommend the immediate mitigation path, then describe its downside and one compensating control to manage that downside.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion
reportSections.template: Close with a concise 24–72 hour outlook, decision trigger points, and who should own the next actions.

gapRules.prompt: Consider including a specific collection plan (one or two concrete actions). Explain what each result would confirm, refute, or re-prioritize.
Consider stating one downside of your chosen mitigation and naming a compensating control that could reduce that downside.
Consider strengthening adversary modeling by explicitly connecting actor intent, capability, and targeting to the evidence trail.
Explain your risk rationale in terms of likely impact, exposure, and attacker opportunity.
Describe why you selected this confidence level. Note what evidence quality limitations or unresolved gaps influenced your decision, and what would increase confidence.
